message.content: Fresh adversarial CI/supply-chain + observability + packaging audit of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (5 passes hardened). ONLY genuine reproducible defects, file:line. Expect little. JSON id (CV-O-N).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me focus on the key audit targets: CI workflow, packaging, and observability code.

content.content:
1	name: ci
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main]
6	  pull_request:
7	
8	# Least privilege (#H-008): the default GITHUB_TOKEN can be repo-write; this workflow only reads
9	# the code and uploads artifacts, so pin the token to read-only at the top level. Any job needing
10	# more must opt in explicitly with its own `permissions:`.
11	permissions:
12	  contents: read
13	
14	jobs:
15	  # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
16	  # (optional backends are import-guarded; integration tests skip). This guards ADR-0006.
17	  core:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    strategy:
20	      matrix:
21	        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
22	    steps:
23	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
24	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
25	        with:
26	          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
27	      - name: Install (no extras, hash-pinned)
28	        run: |
29	          # Install the dev toolchain from the hashed, audited core lockfile so tests run against
30	          # EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned and the SBOM describes — not a live PyPI
31	          # resolution where a malicious patch release could execute under test (#F-004). The
32	          # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
33	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
34	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
35	          pip install --no-deps -e .
36	      - name: Lint (ruff)
37	        run: ruff check src tests
38	      - name: Format check (ruff)
39	        run: ruff format --check src tests
40	      - name: Type-check (mypy)
41	        run: mypy
42	      - name: Test (pytest)
43	        run: pytest -q
44	
45	  # Extras lane: install the real backends and run the integration suite so the backend code
46	  # paths actually execute, and mypy type-checks against the REAL (not ignored) library types.
47	  # Service-dependent tests (ollama/qdrant/postgres) skip when the service is absent in CI; the
48	  # extra-only tests (langgraph graph, mlflow lifecycle, OTel, numpy primitives, methodology
49	  # e2e) run for real.
50	  integration:
51	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
52	    services:
53	      postgres:
54	        image: postgres:16
55	        env:
56	          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
57	          POSTGRES_DB: assay
58	        ports: ["5432:5432"]
59	        options: >-
60	          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U postgres"
61	          --health-interval 5s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 10
62	      qdrant:
63	        image: qdrant/qdrant:v1.12.4
64	        ports: ["6333:6333"]
65	    env:
66	      # loopback host keeps the data-sovereignty guard satisfied; creds are redacted on error
67	      ASSAY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://[REDACTED]@localhost:5432/assay
68	    steps:
69	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
70	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
71	        with:
72	          python-version: "3.12"
73	      - name: Install (all extras, hash-pinned)
74	        run: |
75	          # Install from the hashed all-extras lockfile so the integration suite executes against
76	          # exactly the audited, pinned versions — closing the window where a compromised patch
77	          # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
78	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
79	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
80	          pip install --no-deps -e .
81	      - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
82	        run: mypy
83	      - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
84	        # #H-009: the live Postgres/Qdrant tests SKIP when the service is unreachable, so a
85	        # misconfigured/never-started container would let the integration job report GREEN without
86	        # actually exercising the backends. Fail loudly here if either service isn't up, so the
87	        # gate cannot pass vacuously.
88	        run: |
89	          set -euo pipefail
90	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
91	            pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -U postgres && break || sleep 2
92	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Postgres service never became ready"; exit 1; }
93	          done
94	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
95	            curl -fsS http://localhost:6333/readyz && break || sleep 2
96	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Qdrant service never became ready"; exit 1; }
97	          done
98	          echo "postgres + qdrant reachable"
99	      # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
100	      # numpy primitives, the T3 drain/classification (synthetic SDK, no metered call), AND —
101	      # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
102	      # Ollama and the metered frontier tier are not in CI and skip honestly.
103	      - name: Integration + unit tests with coverage
104	        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing
105	
106	  # Supply-chain: fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship
107	  # with advisories). The scan runs against the committed, fully-pinned, hashed lockfile
108	  # (requirements.lock) so the audit is reproducible across runs and matches what installs
109	  # (#108, #130, #131, #132, #145, #146). An allowlist file (.pip-audit-ignore, one advisory id
110	  # per line, '#' comments and blank lines allowed) covers accepted/unfixable ones.
111	  audit:
112	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
113	    steps:
114	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
115	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
116	        with:
117	          python-version: "3.12"
118	      - name: Install pip-audit
119	        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
120	        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
121	        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
122	        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
123	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
124	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
125	        run: |
126	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
127	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
128	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
129	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
130	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
131	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
132	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
133	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
134	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
135	          # stay in sync too.
136	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
137	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
138	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
139	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
140	        run: |
141	          set -euo pipefail
142	          # --strict: any dependency that cannot be collected/audited FAILS the gate (no silent
143	          # skips). --require-hashes: every pin must carry a hash. Build --ignore-vuln args as an
144	          # array so allowlist entries can't word-split or glob-expand, and read the file
145	          # newline-agnostically so the last line is honored even without a trailing newline.
146	          IGNORES=()
147	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
148	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
149	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
150	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
151	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
152	          fi
153	          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
154	      - name: SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile
155	        run: |
156	          set -euo pipefail
157	          # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
158	          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
159	          # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
160	          # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
161	          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
162	          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
163	          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
164	          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
165	          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
166	          IGNORES=()
167	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
168	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
169	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
170	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
171	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
172	          fi
173	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
174	          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
175	          echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
176	      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
177	        if: always()
178	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
179	        with:
180	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
181	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
182	          if-no-files-found: error
183	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
184	        run: |
185	          set -euo pipefail
186	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
187	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
188	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
189	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
190	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
191	          pip install --no-deps -e .
192	          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
193	          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
194	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
195	      - name: Upload license report
196	        if: always()
197	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
198	        with:
199	          name: license-report
200	          path: licenses.json
201	          if-no-files-found: warn
202	
203	  # Single required status check (#F-014): branch protection should require ONLY `all-checks`.
204	  # It fans in every other job, so adding a new job automatically gates merges without anyone
205	  # remembering to update the branch-protection rule — and `audit`/`integration` can never be
206	  # silently dropped from the merge gate by requiring just `core`.
207	  all-checks:
208	    if: always()
209	    needs: [core, integration, audit]
210	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
211	    steps:
212	      - name: Verify all required jobs succeeded
213	        run: |
214	          results="${{ needs.core.result }} ${{ needs.integration.result }} ${{ needs.audit.result }}"
215	          echo "job results: $results"
216	          for r in $results; do
217	            if [ "$r" != "success" ]; then
218	              echo "::error::a required job did not succeed ($r)"
219	              exit 1
220	            fi
221	          done
222	          echo "all required jobs succeeded"

---LICENSE GATE---
"""License gate (#147): fail if any installed dependency carries a strong-copyleft license.

Reads a pip-licenses JSON report (path as argv[1], default ``licenses.json``) and exits non-zero
if any package's license matches a denied strong-copyleft family that would be incompatible with
the Apache-2.0 distribution. LGPL (weak copyleft, dynamic linking) is permitted. Advisory
monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot (.github/dependabot.yml).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import sys

# Strong-copyleft families denied in the distributed dependency set. Both the SPDX-style short
# tokens AND the full prose names must be listed (pass 3, #F-015): a package reporting
# "Server Side Public License" or "European Union Public Licence 1.2" contains neither the "SSPL"
# nor "EUPL" token, so the short tokens alone let those families bypass the gate.
_DENY = (
    "GPL",
    "AGPL",
    "SSPL",
    "EUPL",
    "SERVER SIDE PUBLIC",  # SSPL prose form
    "EUROPEAN UNION PUBLIC",  # EUPL prose form
    "AFFERO",  # AGPL prose form ("GNU Affero General Public License")
)


def main(path: str = "licenses.json") -> int:
    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:  # context manager — no leaked fd (#F-050)
        packages = json.load(f)
    bad = []
    unknown = []
    for pkg in packages:
        raw = (pkg.get("License") or "").strip()
        lic = raw.upper()
        # A package whose license is empty / UNKNOWN / unspecified could be hiding a copyleft the
        # deny-substring scan can't see. Don't fail on it (pip-licenses reports UNKNOWN for many
        # legitimate packages with incomplete metadata), but SURFACE it as a CI warning so a
        # reviewer checks it instead of it passing silently (#H-007).
        if not raw or lic in ("UNKNOWN", "UNKNOWN LICENSE", "NONE"):
            unknown.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}")
            continue
        if "LGPL" in lic:  # weak copyleft — permitted for dynamically-linked libraries
            continue
        if any(tok in lic for tok in _DENY):
            bad.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}: {pkg.get('License')}")
    if unknown:
        print(
            "::warning::license gate: "
            + str(len(unknown))
            + " package(s) report no/UNKNOWN license — review for hidden copyleft: "
            + ", ".join(sorted(unknown))
        )
    if bad:
        print("::error::strong-copyleft licenses found:\n" + "\n".join(bad))
        return 1
    print(f"license gate OK ({len(packages)} packages checked, {len(unknown)} unknown)")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "licenses.json"))

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Regenerate the hash-pinned lockfiles from pyproject.toml (#G-016).
#
# Dependabot bumps the version ranges in pyproject.toml but cannot regenerate the uv lockfiles,
# so a Dependabot PR will fail the CI "requirements.lock is stale" gate until the lockfiles are
# regenerated. Run this on a Dependabot branch (or any PR that changes pyproject dependencies),
# then commit the updated requirements*.lock files:
#
#     scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh && git commit -am "chore: regenerate lockfiles"
#
# uv is pinned to the same version CI uses (#F-031) so the output matches the sync check exactly.
set -euo pipefail

UV_VERSION="0.11.21"

if ! command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ "$(uv --version | awk '{print $2}')" != "$UV_VERSION" ]; then
  echo "installing uv==$UV_VERSION (CI-pinned) ..." >&2
  python -m pip install "uv==$UV_VERSION" >&2
fi

uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock
uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock

echo "regenerated requirements.lock + requirements-core.lock — review and commit them."

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.

Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it — an auditable record alongside the work.

What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.

Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).

Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
commit).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import datetime as _dt
import hashlib
import json
import os
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"

# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
# for the full public-release consideration.
_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED]    # The Claude project-dir SLUG (e.g. -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc): redact the username segment
    # regardless of length (#H-018) — the bare-token rule below skips short handles to avoid
    # over-redacting common words, but the slug form is unambiguous PII at any length.
    re.compile(r"(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/\"']+"),  # -Users-[REDACTED]- → -Users-[REDACTED]-
]


def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).

    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
    """
    try:
        name = Path.home().name
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
        return None
    if not name or len(name) < 4:
        return None
    return re.compile(re.escape(name))


_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()

"""assay_engine — the reusable, dataset-agnostic engine of the hc-assay blueprint.

The single architectural rule (ADR-0002, ARCHITECTURE.md §3):

    The engine never imports dataset specifics. Adapters implement the interfaces in
    ``assay_engine.contracts`` that the engine calls. A clone is: implement the adapter,
    register it, run.

This package contains only goal-agnostic machinery:

- ``contracts``      — the engine ↔ adapter boundary (canonical schema + adapter Protocols).
- ``methodology``    — hypotheses, the three verdicts, the two firewalls, the
                       measurement↔interpretation fence, confirmatory-test machinery.
- ``baseline``       — dataset-agnostic primitives (similarity/distance, descriptive stats) and
                       a determinism/reproducibility harness — the raw material; choice-bearing
                       builders (embeddings, clustering, graph/topology) are supplied per study by
                       the adapter's BaselineBuilder (ADR-0002).
- ``orchestration``  — the analysis graph, phase machine, and governance gates.
- ``reasoning``      — the tiered LLM reasoning seam.
- ``observability``  — self-hosted tracing + experiment tracking.

1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))
31	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
32	
33	_provider: Any = None
34	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
35	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
36	
37	
38	def _disabled() -> bool:
39	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
40	
41	
42	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
43	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
44	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
45	
46	
47	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
48	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
49	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
50	
51	
52	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54	
55	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57	    """
58	    global _provider
59	    if _disabled():
60	        return None
61	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62	    with _provider_lock:
63	        if _provider is not None:
64	            return _provider
65	        try:
66	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69	            from phoenix.otel import register
70	
71	            provider = register(
72	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
75	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
76	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
77	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
78	            )
79	            _provider = provider
80	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
81	            _instrument_langchain()
82	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
83	            return _provider
84	        except Exception as exc:
85	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced. Graceful
86	            # degradation, but no longer SILENT (pass 3, #F-044): a misconfigured
87	            # ASSAY_TRACING_HOST/PORT or a missing observability extra now leaves a diagnosable
88	            # warning instead of an inexplicably untraced run.
89	            _log.warning(
90	                "assay tracing: setup failed, running untraced (%s: %s)",
91	                type(exc).__name__,
92	                exc,
93	            )
94	            return None
95	
96	
97	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
98	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
99	    try:
100	        from opentelemetry import trace
101	
102	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
103	            _log.warning(
104	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
105	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
106	            )
107	    except Exception:
108	        pass
109	
110	
111	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
112	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
113	    try:
114	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
115	
116	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
117	    except Exception:
118	        pass
119	
120	
121	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
122	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
123	
124	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
125	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
126	    """
127	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
128	    if not callable(flush):
129	        return
130	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
131	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
132	        try:
133	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
134	
135	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
136	                _safe(flush)
137	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
138	                if callable(prior):
139	                    prior(signum, frame)
140	
141	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
142	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
143	            pass
144	
145	
146	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
147	    try:
148	        fn()
149	    except Exception:
150	        pass
151	
152	
153	@contextmanager
154	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
155	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
156	
157	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
158	    """
159	    if run_id is None:
160	        yield
161	        return
162	    try:
163	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
164	    except ImportError:
165	        yield
166	        return
167	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
168	    try:
169	        yield
170	    finally:
171	        context.detach(token)
172	
173	
174	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
175	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
176	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
177	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
178	
179	
180	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
181	class Tracer(Protocol):
182	    @contextmanager
183	    def span(
184	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
185	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
186	
187	
188	class OtelTracer:
189	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
190	
191	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
192	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
193	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
194	    """
195	
196	    @contextmanager
197	    def span(
198	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
199	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
200	        try:
201	            from opentelemetry import trace
202	        except ImportError:
203	            yield
204	            return
205	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
206	        # start_as_current_span defaults record_exception=True and set_status_on_exception=True,
207	        # so an exception escaping the body is automatically recorded as a span event and the span
208	        # status is set to ERROR with the exception text (verified in test_observability) — #F-005
209	        # was a false positive; no manual error-recording is needed here.
210	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
211	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
212	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
213	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
214	            yield
215	
216	
217	class UnconfiguredTracer:
218	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
219	
220	    @contextmanager
221	    def span(
222	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
223	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
224	        raise NotImplementedError(
225	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
226	            "test double"
227	        )
228	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        # The run now exists in the store. If param-logging fails partway (network blip, server
75	        # error), the run would otherwise be orphaned permanently in RUNNING state — phantom runs
76	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
77	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
78	        try:
79	            for k, v in dict(params).items():
80	                client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
81	        except Exception:
82	            try:
83	                client.set_terminated(run_id, status="FAILED")
84	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 - best-effort cleanup; original error re-raised
85	                pass
86	            raise
87	        return run_id
88	
89	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
90	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
91	
92	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
93	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
94	
95	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
96	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
97	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
98	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)

findings.title: PII/secret redaction is bypassed for every non-.jsonl transcript file (operator email + username already leaked into committed transcripts/)
findings.description:
`_copy_scrubbed` only runs the `_scrub` redaction when `srcf.suffix == ".jsonl"` (line 104-105). Every other captured file type — `.json`, `.meta.json`, `.txt` (tool-results), `.js` (workflow scripts) — falls to the `else` branch and is copied byte-for-byte verbatim (`data = raw`, line 107). The redaction patterns added in hardening passes (#F-024 home-path/email masking, #G-007 bare-username, #H-018 project-dir slug, plus the credential-shaped secret patterns) therefore never run on the bulk of the captured corpus.

The pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) `git add`s the result of this script, and the script's own docstring states the repo may be published. The bypass is not theoretical: the already-committed transcripts/ tree contains, in non-.jsonl files only, 54,299 unredacted `/Users/[REDACTED] path occurrences (json:2487, js:36, txt:51776 — zero in .jsonl, proving the scrub works there and is skipped elsewhere), 82 occurrences of `[REDACTED]`, and 10 of `[REDACTED]` — exactly the operator identity #F-024/#G-007 were written to mask. .jsonl files show 0 leaks, confirming the gate is the cause, not the patterns.

This turns a stated, hardened security/provenance control into a silent no-op for the file types that dominate the snapshot, and has already published operator PII to the tracked tree.

findings.suggested_fix: Scrub by content, not by suffix. Apply `_scrub` to all UTF-8-decodable text files (the capture only ever ingests text transcripts/metadata/scripts), e.g. replace the suffix gate at lines 104-107 with an attempt to decode-and-scrub every file and fall back to raw bytes only on a UnicodeDecodeError (genuine binary). Concretely: `try: data = _scrub(raw.decode('utf-8')).encode('utf-8') except UnicodeDecodeError: data = raw`. Then re-run the capture (or a one-off scrub pass) over the existing transcripts/ tree and commit the redacted result, and consider `git filter-repo` to purge the leaked PII from history before any public release.
